mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is flat, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum that supports near-term equity price appreciation over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation expectations exceed 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate income, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases consistently coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing real discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for Fed forward guidance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside a stable or declining unemployment rate signals synchronized strength in consumer confidence and labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns when HOUST increases but UNRATE remains flat, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings trajectories, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% coincides with negative 60-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of observations, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during late-cycle phases.
